generated_oml:
// ========================= Enablers =========================
instance MqttBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker used to receive sensor streams from the robot"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, ModelCalibrationService
]

instance SimulinkEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink engine executing multi‑physics simulations of robot dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:enables InsightGenerationService
]

instance MLEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine‑learning runtime executing regression models trained on sensor traces"
    DTDFVocab:enables InsightGenerationService
]

// ========================= Services =========================
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that monitors live angle and current streams for anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyImprovementInsight
]

instance ModelCalibrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that calibrates simulation and ML models using incoming sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCoefficientInsight
]

instance InsightGenerationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that aggregates processed results and generates actionable recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:provides AdjustControlParametersAction
]

// ========================= Insights & Actions =========================
instance FrictionCoefficientInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Estimated joint friction coefficients derived from calibration runs"
]

instance MotionAccuracyImprovementInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of positioning accuracy improvements after model updates"
]

instance AdjustControlParametersAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic adjustment of controller gains based on identified friction and accuracy insights"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// ========================= Models =========================
instance SimulinkMultiPhysicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi‑physics model of the six‑axis robot implemented in Simulink"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEngine
]

instance MLRegressionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data‑driven regression model trained on sensor traces for friction estimation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MLEngine
]

// ========================= Sensors, Data Transmitted & Data =========================
// Axis 1
instance MotorEncoderAxis1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor‑mounted encoder on Axis 1 delivering angular position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DataTransmittedMotorEncoderAngleAxis1
]

instance GearboxEncoderAxis1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearbox output encoder on Axis 1 delivering angular position after the gear train"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DataTransmittedGearboxEncoderAngleAxis1
]

instance MotorCurrentSensorAxis1 : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input electric current of the motor on Axis 1"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DataTransmittedMotorCurrentAxis1
]

instance DataTransmittedMotorEncoderAngleAxis1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoderAxis1
]

instance DataTransmittedGearboxEncoderAngleAxis1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxEncoderAxis1
]

instance DataTransmittedMotorCurrentAxis1 : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorCurrentSensorAxis1
]

instance MotorEncoderAngleDataAxis1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Angular position time‑series from MotorEncoderAxis1"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DataTransmittedMotorEncoderAngleAxis1
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MqttBroker
]

instance GearboxEncoderAngleDataAxis1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Angular position time‑series from GearboxEncoderAxis1"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DataTransmittedGearboxEncoderAngleAxis1
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MqttBroker
]

instance MotorCurrentDataAxis1 : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Electric current time‑series from MotorCurrentSensorAxis1"
    DTDFVocab:fromData DataTransmittedMotorCurrentAxis1
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MqttBroker
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is orchestrated according to the Digital Shadow reference model (Fig. 1), which is expressed as a UML class diagram that follows a component‑connector architecture. Core elements include Data Trace objects that hold contextual data and metadata, a Processing Component that consumes input Data Traces, selects an appropriate Simulation Model or Data‑Calculation Model (e.g., a Simulink model, a C++ algorithm, or a state‑machine defined in SysML), executes the model, and emits a new Data Trace. The origin‑from association links every generated Data Trace to the responsible Processing Component, the input traces, and the specific model, preserving transitive traceability across the chain. The architecture is aligned with Model‑Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) layers—requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and physical/technical layer—and may be described with SysML, ADLs such as AADL, MontiArc, or ACME, and integrated with external simulation tools. Resource management and coordination are handled by the Processing Component, which schedules model execution, manages data flow, and ensures that the produced Data Traces conform to the semantic contracts defined by the reference model, thereby constituting the overall DT constellation."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The methodology emphasizes model fidelity by integrating physics‑based white‑box simulations with data‑driven black‑box models and requires validation through comparison of simulated outputs against measured sensor data. Traceability is provided by the Digital Shadow Reference Model, which records model versions and transformation steps (e.g., from SysML to simulation code). The literature highlights the need to address uncertainty sources such as modeling errors and sensor noise, and calls for uncertainty quantification, error detection, and correction mechanisms, together with systematic verification of model‑to‑code pipelines to ensure quality and correctness."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection relies on the MQTT messaging protocol for the one‑way data transmission from the physical object to the digital representation. No additional details on network topology, bandwidth, latency, redundancy, or security protocols are provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The implementation conforms to several industry standards: the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) is used as the information model for asset metadata; system architecture and behavior are modeled with SysML (including block definition diagrams and internal block diagrams) and expressed in UML class diagrams as the Digital Shadow Reference Model; ADLs such as ACME or AADL are referenced for component‑connector descriptions. These standards provide a certification‑ready, interoperable foundation and enable integration with other AAS‑compatible tools."
]
